channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump begins firings of FAA staff just weeks after fatal DC plane crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has begun firing several hundred Federal Aviation Administration employees, upending staff on a busy air travel weekend and just weeks after a January fatal midair collision at Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Rwanda-backed M23 rebels tighten their grip on second major city in eastern Congo
item.description: BUKAVU, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels tightened their grip on Bukavu on Monday, a day after seizing the second major city in eastern Congo whose residents appeared resigned to their fate under the new rulers.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL CINAMULA and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: European leaders want a say in talks to end Russia's war in Ukraine. The US is brushing them off
item.description: PARIS (AP) — European leaders insisted Monday they must have a say in international talks to end the war in Ukraine despite the clear message from both Washington and Moscow that there was no role for them as yet in negotiations that could shape the future of the continent.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Illinois family suffers loss as home explodes on wedding day
item.description: “I ran here in my wedding dress, like down the alley, and was watching from a neighbor’s yard,” said Eleni Vrettos, of Cicero. “Everything was just smoke at that point.
item.dc:creator: Courtney Spinelli, NewsNation

item.title: Crews rescue teen from 50-foot-deep California mineshaft: Watch
item.description: Placer County, California, rescue crews pounced into action and extracted a 16-year-old boy who was stranded in a nearly 50-foot mine shaft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How to file your taxes for free
item.description: Filing a tax return doesn't have to cost you money. While some may hire a tax preparation expert or purchase online software, millions of taxpayers can file state and federal returns for free. Here are some options to file your taxes for free before the April 15 deadline.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KTLA 5

item.title: Gov. Ron DeSantis' former chief of staff James Uthmeier sworn in as Florida’s attorney general
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis named his former chief of staff, James Uthmeier, as the state’s attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Stephany Matat And Kate Payne, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NYC Mayor Eric Adams refuses to stand down amid protests
item.description: The mayor says he will run for reelection and fulfill "the mission that God put me on many years ago.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Becker, NewsNation

item.title: Police arrest apparent leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multiple killings in the US
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The apparent leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians has been arrested in Maryland along with another member of the group, Maryland State Police said Monday. Jack Lasota, 34, was arrested Sunday along with Michelle Zajko, 33, of Media, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, KRON4

item.title: Dell secures deal worth more than $5B in Elon Musk's xAI startup company
item.description: Elon Musk's xAI is one step closer to becoming a reality after Dell secured a deal worth more than $5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, NewsNation

item.title: Trump suggests no laws violated if he ‘saves’ country
item.description: Trump doubled down on the Napoleon reference on Sunday, reposting an image of the French emperor riding into battle with the text "He who saves his country violates no law" overlaid.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron speaks to NewsNation after Daytona 500 win
item.description: Byron made sure to celebrate his victory, sleeping for an hour before returning to Daytona International Speedway on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron's cause of death revealed
item.description: The cause of death of South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron has been revealed. The 24-year-old's death has been ruled a suicide by the Seongdong Police Station in Seoul, according to The Korea Times.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: FAA firings begin weeks after deadly crash near DC
item.description: The Trump administration has begun firing hundreds of employees at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) just weeks after a deadly crash outside of Washington, D.C., put a spotlight on the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: NAACP spending guide urges Black Americans to avoid companies that dumped DEI
item.description: The nation’s oldest civil rights organization released its Black Consumer Advisory, a project intended to help educate Black Americans on “who’s pushing progress and who’s stuck in the past.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Cruise ship carrying 169 norovirus patients docks in Florida
item.description: A cruise ship carrying 169 passengers and crew members sick with norovirus docked in Florida on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Elderly couple unexpectedly finds love again in Rhode Island
item.description: Judy Furtado, 80, and George Denicourt, 82, have been going strong ever since the two met at the Johnston Senior Center.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Abbott holds briefing on severe weather preparations across the state
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott on Monday morning will hold a press conference in Austin on Texas' preparation and response ahead of winter weather set to impact the state.
item.dc:creator: Adam Schwager, NewsNation

item.title: Pope remains in hospital to treat 'complex clinical' condition: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis’ respiratory tract infection is presenting a “complex clinical picture” that will require further hospitalization, the Vatican said Monday as concerns grew about the increasingly frail health of the 88-year-old pontiff.
item.dc:creator: By NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fire at Washington state indoor gun range leaves 1 dead, 1 injured
item.description: The Tacoma Fire Department responded to a deadly fire at the Bull's Eye Indoor Range on Sunday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump names Jim Byron to senior archivist role
item.description: “Jim will manage the National Archives on a day-to-day basis, while we continue our search for a full-time Archivist,” Trump posted.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: 3 dead in fiery Wyoming tunnel crash
item.description: Another person has been confirmed dead as the investigation continues into a major, multi-vehicle crash in the westbound tunnel of I-80 in Green River, Wyoming, on Friday, according to the Wyoming Dept. of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WGN 9

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KRON4

item.title: Mother of man tortured and killed in New York speaks out
item.description: “They are scum, they are evil, I don't know how somebody can be that evil," Linda Nordquist said of the people accused in the death of her son, Sam.
item.dc:creator: Adam Chodak, KRON4

item.title: Transgender man found dead in New York field endured repeated acts torture: police
item.description: “This is one of the most horrific crimes I've ever investigated,” Captain Kelly Swift of the New York State Police Bureau of Criminal Investigation said.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Dodgers star Shohei Ohtani throws 1st BP of spring training, taking a step toward possibly pitching
item.description: Los Angeles Dodgers superstar Shohei Ohtani threw his first bullpen of spring training on Saturday, taking a step toward possibly pitching for the first time since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What does 'Wawa' even mean? The company offers up a hint
item.description: In certain communities along the East Coast, the mere mention of the name “Wawa” conjures up visions of piping-hot coffee, scratch-off lottery tickets and pre-made Sizzli breakfast sandwiches.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 people shot in Philadelphia as city celebrates Eagles victory
item.description: Two people were shot in Philadelphia Friday afternoon as the city held its Eagles Super Bowl Parade, Philadelphia Police said. The shooting happened outside of the parade footprint, police said.
item.dc:creator: Jared Weaver, Fox 5
